authorityEvidence.authorityLimit: The report records upstream, clonePath, cloneHead, and dirty files, but it is not an independent repository identity proof.
The instruction pack names work/external/escape-html as the target, but it does not make the path portable or supported by the compiler.
Graph source context explains the retrofit target but does not grant execution authority or graph delta apply authority.

authorityChecks.reason: Static dogfood metadata and the calibration fixture both identify component/escape-html.
Static dogfood metadata and the instruction pack both declare work/external/escape-html.
The dogfood report records cloneHead b42947eefa79efff01b3fe988c4c7e7b051ec8d8, but the compiler does not independently verify this checkout.
The dogfood report records index.js and test/index.js as dirty files, which is useful for calibration but not support authority.

unresolvedAuthorityGaps.reason: The compiler has not independently verified remote URL, clone head, or checkout contents from the external repository.
work/external/escape-html is a local developer checkout path and cannot be assumed to exist in other workspaces.
The observed checkout is intentionally dirty for dogfood evidence and cannot be treated as a clean repository baseline.
Checkout authority preview does not permit command execution, file modification, candidate generation, or promotion approval.

allowedUse: Explain the source context for external required-check binding previews.
Tie observed dogfood Evidence to the declared component/escape-html local checkout.
Report why external check bindings remain calibration-only and non-portable.
Support future scope discussion for external checkout authority without declaring support.
forbiddenUse: Do not treat this path as a supported compiler input.
Do not execute npm test or any external command as a required check from this preview.
Do not modify files in the external checkout from this preview.
Do not treat this preview as CI enforcement, GitHub required checks, branch protection, promotion approval, user acceptance, or graph delta apply authority.

effectOnRequiredCheckBinding.reason: The checkout path is now described as calibration-local source context, but it is still non-portable and not authoritative enough for supported required-check binding.

recommendedNextStep.summary: Decide whether graph-delta review binding should be the next v0.3 calibration scope.
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Behavior-change policy, external required-check binding, checkout path authority, anchor-level context, and risk vocabulary now have preview boundaries; the remaining calibration gap is graph-delta review binding.

nonExecutionBoundary: This checkout authority preview describes static metadata for a calibration-local external checkout only. It does not mark the second fixture as supported, wire it into compile-contract --dry-run, execute checks, modify files, compile a candidate, create or approve a promotion packet, create CI enforcement, create GitHub required checks, configure branch protection, apply graph deltas, grant execution authority, automate user acceptance, or retire tree-native artifacts.
